location.location: San Francisco, California, United States of America

education.field: Symbolic Systems (Human-Computer Interaction, Psychology, Philosophy, Computer Science)

experience.title: Co-founder & CTO
experience.description: Kevin Systrom & I started Instagram in mid-2010, and both designed & developed the application. I've spent the last few years scaling the Instagram engineering team into a 450+ person organization, now within FB.

experience.title: User Experience Designer & Engineer
experience.description: Prototyped & tested several new products. Later transitioned to designing & developing the internal & external stats and visualization tools for our partners.

experience.title: Advising Fellow
experience.description: Organizing events, presentations, and other activities related to Symbolic Systems, a Stanford interdisciplinary program that unites Cognitive Science, Psychology, Computer Science, Philosophy, and Linguistics. Holding office hours for current majors and prospective students to receive advice about the major and related classes.

experience.title: Oral Communications Tutor
experience.description: One-on-one sessions with students who have upcoming presentations in their classes, as well as class visits to explain broader points about Oral Communications. Also, through this position and my work at Microsoft, I've been invited to speak at Oral Communications-centered courses on the art and science of visual aids and effective use of slideware.

experience.title: Product Development Intern
experience.description: Designing and implementing features for next-gen search engine. Took features from paper prototypes to implementation in Python/JS/HTML.

experience.title: Program Manager Intern
experience.description: Coordinated feature teams (Program Manager, Test, and Development) to implement and polish features in PowerPoint and OfficeArt. Work focused on accessibility, photo import with the Photo Album, and successful management of features as product moved towards ship date. Several features already shipped as part of the Beta 2 Technical Refresh.

school.name: Simmons College - Simmons School of Management

experience.title: Chief Financial Officer
experience.description: Led the finance function for Cedar, an innovative healthcare payments and patient engagement platform. Strategic finance partner to sales, delivery, product & engineering, marketing, and HR functions. Operational leadership over efficiency initiatives and client service center.

experience.title: Global Head of Corporate & Business Development
experience.description:
Capital Markets & Investor Relations: Leadership role in Airbnb’s $3.7bn IPO including drafting of S-1 and roadshow materials, transaction structuring, investor Q&A preparation, and valuation analysis. Led board pricing committee meetings and investor allocation process. Subsequently led $2bn zero coupon convertible note transaction, Airbnb’s first capital raise as a public company. As a private company: (i) led $2bn term loan issuance during pandemic (April 2020); (ii) co-led $1bn Series F (primary capital & tender offer); (iii) completed closing of $1.5bn Series E; and (iv) secured $1bn corporate revolver.

Corporate & Business Development: Built and led team responsible for thesis development, due diligence, execution, and integration of more than 10 acquisitions. Built transaction execution and strategic partnerships teams, deal committee and board review process, and acquisition integration management function.

experience.title: Director, FP&A
experience.description:
Corporate FP&A: Led FP&A team responsible for: (i) P&L consolidation and budget variance analysis; (ii) revenue forecasting; (iii) Airbnb’s annual planning process; and (iv) investor and board reporting. 
 
Operations FP&A: Led FP&A team responsible for analytics and budgeting for: (i) community support; (ii) trust & safety; (iii) regulation & public policy; and (iv) Airbnb’s businesses in China and India.

experience.title: Principal, Private Equity Group
experience.description:
Evaluated and executed control transactions and minority investments in private and public companies within the consumer/retail, business services, and financial technology sectors.

Board member of Service King Collision Repair, Performance Food Group (NYSE:PFGC), and GCA Services; board observer of Crocs, Inc (NASDAQ:CROX).

unstandardized_experience__internal.description: Led the finance function for Cedar, an innovative healthcare payments and patient engagement platform. experience.title: CEO and Co-Founder
experience.description: Combining scaling and long-term AI alignment methods to build steerable, interpretable, and robust AI systems.

experience.title: Vice President of Research
experience.description:
* I led the efforts to build GPT-2 and GPT-3 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2005.14165).
* I am one of two people who sets overall research direction at OpenAI and writes its annual research roadmap.
* In addition I have built and lead several teams focused on long-term safety research, including how to make AI systems more interpretable and how to embed human preferences and values in future powerful AI systems.

experience.title: Senior Research Scientist
experience.description: I worked as a deep learning researcher on the Google Brain team, working to extend the capabilities of neural networks.  I have also worked on the safety and reliability of AI systems, and recently published a paper (with Chris Olah and several other researchers) laying out some key problems for preventing accidents in AI systems.  Our work (https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.06565) is described here (https://www.technologyreview.com/s/601750/google-gets-practical-about-the-dangers-of-ai/).

experience.title: Research Scientist
experience.description:
I work with Andrew Ng and a small team of AI scientists and systems engineers to solve hard problems in deep learning and AI, including speech recognition and natural language processing.

* Performed the majority of machine learning research for Deep Speech 2 (http://arxiv.org/abs/1512.02595), listed by MIT Technology Review as one of the top 10 technological breakthroughs of 2016 (https://www.technologyreview.com/s/600766/10-breakthrough-technologies-2016-conversational-interfaces/)
* Conceived, prototyped and implemented a neural network architecture that achieved a 35% relative reduction in the word error rate (WER) of the lab's English speech system.
* Conceived, prototyped and implemented a neural network architecture that achieved a 15% relative reduction in the WER of the lab's Chinese speech system.
* Increased speed of decoder by 10x in Chinese and English, removing a key obstacle to deployment of the lab's speech system to Baidu users.
* Increased speed of training for Chinese system by 27% and reduced memory usage by 50%, resulting in a substantial increase in lab-wide productivity and computational efficiency.
* Co-developer (with Jim Fan and Jesse Engel) of Baidu's internal neural network library
* Awarded "Star Employee of the Quarter" for Q2 2015.

experience.title: Software Developer (part-time)
experience.description:
Skyline is a comprehensive software suite essential to the research of a large fraction of the proteomics community: 30,000 unique installs, 7000 instances/week, 300K lines of C# code, 10 professional programmers
• Personally contributed 14K lines of code (net, human-generated) + 50 pages tutorials and docs.
• Invented and implemented peak picking, binary classification, Storey-Tipshirani, and signal processing algorithms for mass spectrometry data, now widely employed by Skyline’s user base.
• Instructor at weeklong course (Zurich, February 2014) attended by 40 on how to use Skyline, invited to teach second course in Barcelona (October 2014).
• Featured speaker at Skyline user group meeting (June 2014, attendees: 250), and IMSC Work-
shop (August 2014, attendees: 100+)
• Regularly handle support requests from Skyline users (3-4 requests/week)

experience.title: Postdoctoral Scholar
experience.description:
- Researching improved computational methods for acquisition and analysis of high-throughput protein expression data (C#, Python, Matlab).
- Invented, developed, and implemented protein detection and statistical confidence algorithms and tools that have driven the field-wide adoption of next-generation high throughput mass spectrometry.
- Developed multiplexing technique that increases peptides detected per run by 25%.
- Contributed to open source proteomics software projects, including Skyline and Proteowizard.
- Developed method for distinguishing cancer cells from normal cells using biophysical modeling and linear classification.

experience.title: Consulant
experience.description:
• Help invent and develop breakthrough products for industry and government.
• Design and conduct simulations to address unusually difficult physics and math problems. 
• Experience with electromagnetic devices, complex mechanical systems.

experience.title: PhD Student in Computational Neuroscience
experience.description:
- Dissertation awarded Hertz Doctoral Thesis Prize, 2012.
- Co-authored grant proposal leading to McKnight Technological Innovations in Neuroscience Award (4 awards given out of 97 proposals).
- Developed Hopfield-style statistical models for real (biological) neural networks in retina and cortex (implemented in C/Matlab).
- Helped develop novel algorithms to solve neural mixed-signal (spike sorting) problems at 300 neuron scale (C/C++).  Performed first ever recording of a complete 0.5x0.5mm patch of retina.
- Co-invented and developed novel device for in vivo intracellular recording, personally built initial prototypes in fabrication facility.

experience.title: Geophysicist
experience.description:
• Invented and developed highly mathematical techniques in computational seismology. 
• Published non-confidential portions of my research in Physical Review E. 
• Sole author on Schlumberger confidential report (35 pages) on remaining research.
• Wrote 5000 lines of Matlab, Fortran to implement theoretical work.

experience.title: Research Intern
experience.description:
• Designed robust, fault-tolerant, highly mathematical algorithms for parallel distributed systems. 
• Participated actively and independently in whole development pipeline, from theoretical algo-
rithm development to software simulation to building a physical network of microcontrollers. 
• Results published in confidential AMI report.
